message:
feat(server): bounded backup rotation + narrow test-fixture path guard + cargo-test-harness predicate

Post-mortem response to the 2026-04-19 archive pollution incident, where
the production storage root had accumulated:
  - 1576+ test-fixture project roots leaked from integration tests that
    bypassed the ephemeral-storage reroute
  - 25 `storage.sqlite3.corrupt-*` backups,
    17 `storage.sqlite3.reconstruct-failed-*` backups,
    40+ `storage.sqlite3.archive-reconcile-*` backups
    totaling ~1.3 GB in a single storage_root with no retention policy
Three cooperating pieces landed here; each is independently useful but
they're committed together because they were designed as one defense:

================================================================
1) Bounded backup rotation — NEW crate module
================================================================

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/backup_rotation.rs` (462 lines,
new file; `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`)

- `classify_backup_file(name) -> Option<BackupKind>` buckets a storage-
  root file by filename suffix pattern. Kinds are independent so a
  corruption storm can't starve retention for reconstruction or
  archive-reconcile backups.
- `rotate_storage_root_backups(root, keep_per_kind)` keeps the `N` most
  recent of each kind by mtime and deletes the rest. Default
  `DEFAULT_KEEP_PER_KIND = 3`, overridable via `AM_BACKUP_KEEP_COUNT`
  with a `MIN_KEEP_PER_KIND = 1` floor.
- Classification explicitly refuses to match the live DB
  (`storage.sqlite3`), the Codex sidecar DB, or anything whose stem
  isn't a recognized backup suffix, so rotation can't ever claim the
  production DB as a backup even under adversarial filename crafting.
- Structured tracing at `debug` for individual deletions and `info`
  for rotation summaries so operators can audit what was reaped.
- Module wired into `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` via
  `pub mod backup_rotation;`. The callsite that invokes the rotation
  is not included here — it's landing in the next incident-response
  commit along with the doctor orphan-refs scan.

================================================================
2) Narrow test-fixture path predicate (ephemeral-pollution guard)
================================================================

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/ephemeral.rs` (+257 lines)

Unlike the pre-existing broad `classify_ephemeral` (which drives the
ephemeral-storage reroute), the new `is_ephemeral_project_path`
predicate is intentionally narrow: it fires only on path segments
whose *shape* is unmistakably test-harness output. It is the second
line of defense against call sites that bypass the reroute (direct
`queries::ensure_project` calls, benchmarks, embeddable library
consumers).

Four pattern detectors, each drawn directly from the concrete
fixture leaks in the 2026-04-19 archive dump:

- `looks_like_mkdtemp_segment`: Python `tempfile.mkdtemp()` /
  Rust `tempfile::TempDir` outputs — `tmp` prefix + 6-10
  lowercase-alphanumerics + optional `-`-suffixed metadata.
  Examples seen in the archive: `tmp0jdv7c`, `tmpa0tiqx-project-root`,
  `tmp23baj9-project-root`. Rejects mixed-case false positives like
  `tmpOverride` / `tmpFixtureConfig` that are real directory names,
  not mkdtemp output.
- `looks_like_go_test_segment`: Go `testing.T.TempDir()` /
  `TestXxx<timestamp>` format. Examples: `TestInitCmd_NoArgs2809542922`,
  `TestHandleListMailProjects_Branch2592788348`.
- (additional patterns for pytest fixture paths and nextest scratch
  dirs — see the module header for the full classification)

================================================================
3) Cargo test-harness predicate
================================================================

`crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs` (+278 lines)

`is_running_under_cargo_test_harness()` returns true iff the current
process (or a parent whose env was inherited) is running under a
cargo integration-test / cargo-nextest / insta-snapshot harness.
Used by `guard_against_default_storage_root_in_test_mode` to refuse
the fall-through to the real user storage root — under any test
harness, if the config path resolves to the default user root that's
a leak and the guard makes it a compile-time-ish panic.

Deliberately narrow set of marker env vars:
- `CARGO_TARGET_TMPDIR` — cargo sets this for each integration test
  in `tests/`. Inherited by any subprocess the test spawns, which
  is the exact leak scenario we're defending against.
- `NEXTEST_RUN_ID` — cargo-nextest sets this for the duration of a
  nextest run and propagates it to test binaries.
- `INSTA_WORKSPACE_ROOT` — insta snapshot runner marker.

Intentionally EXCLUDES `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`. That's set by plain
`cargo test` / `cargo run` / build scripts too, so using it would
trigger the guard in normal developer workflows.

================================================================
4) Dependency changes (Cargo.lock)
================================================================

`Cargo.lock`

- `mcp-agent-mail-core` gains `git2` (needed by the git2-backed
  branch of git_cmd, landing in the next incident-response commit
  but pulled in early so the lockfile stays consistent)
- `mcp-agent-mail-core` and `mcp-agent-mail-db` gain `tracing` so
  the new guard / probe paths can emit structured spans

No version bumps of unrelated crates.

================================================================
5) Module wiring: mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs
================================================================

Purely declarative additions:
  `pub mod backup_rotation;`

The remainder of the 55/49 diff on this file is rustfmt-driven
reflow of pre-existing closure blocks (no behavior changes) that
rustfmt picked up incidentally during the work. Bundling it here
rather than in a separate "chore(fmt):" commit avoids churning the
same file twice back-to-back.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
